build_pages.h1: How to Reduce Your Credit Card Processing Fees to 0%
build_pages.win_angle: Rate Saver — only 0% credit answer in market. GoDaddy handles state compliance. No competitor matches.

claim_flags.note: 0% credit processing — confirm Registry #2 + eligibility caveat before publish

build_pages.meta_title: How to Get 0% Credit Card Processing | GoDaddy Rate Saver
build_pages.meta_description: Pass credit card fees to customers legally. Rate Saver takes your credit processing cost to 0% on eligible plans. GoDaddy handles state compliance automatically.
build_pages.not_best_for: Ecommerce-only merchants — Rate Saver not available on Online Store, Appointments, or WooCommerce. Not available in CT, MA, or Puerto Rico.
footnotes: ¹ Rate Saver requires Point of Sale Plus or Invoicing & Pay Links Plus plan. Not available in CT, MA, or Puerto Rico. Not available for ecommerce transactions.

faq.q: Can I pass credit card fees to my customers legally?
faq.a: Yes in most U.S. states. GoDaddy Rate Saver adds a compliant surcharge to credit card transactions automatically. Not available in CT, MA, or Puerto Rico.

faq.q: Does Rate Saver work with debit cards?
faq.a: Rate Saver applies a discounted debit rate (1.9% + $0 in-person) — surcharging debit is not permitted by card networks.

faq.q: What states allow credit card surcharging?
faq.a: Most U.S. states allow surcharging. It is prohibited in CT, MA, and Puerto Rico. GoDaddy handles compliance automatically.

faq.q: Do I need a special plan to use Rate Saver?
faq.a: Yes — Rate Saver requires the Point of Sale Plus or Invoicing & Pay Links Plus plan.

faq.q: Does Rate Saver work for my online store?
faq.a: No — Rate Saver is not available for Online Store, Appointments, or WooCommerce transactions.

faq.q: How much can I save with Rate Saver at $15,000/month?
faq.a: At $15K/month in credit card volume on the standard 2.3% rate, Rate Saver saves approximately $345/month. Results vary by transaction mix. [CLAIM: verify savings math before publish]

faq.q: What happens if a customer refuses to pay the surcharge?
faq.a: You can offer a cash or debit discount as an alternative. The surcharge only applies to credit card transactions.

amplify_threads.thread: Square just raised my fees — what should I switch to?
amplify_threads.draft:
Square's Feb 2025 rate increase (now 2.6% + $0.15 in-person) is pushing a lot of merchants to look around — you're not alone.

If your sales are mostly in-person, GoDaddy Payments is worth a serious look:
- In-person rate: 2.3% + $0 fixed fee on POS Plus [CLAIM: Registry #1]
- $0 fixed fee vs Square's $0.15 = ~$22.50 savings per $15K in volume just on the fixed fee
- Rate Saver: 0% on eligible credit transactions [CLAIM: Registry #2] — pass the fee to customers legally
- No contract, 24/7 phone support on all plans

godaddy.com/payments

Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments.

amplify_threads.thread: Can I pass credit card fees to my customers legally?

claim_flags.note: CT, MA, PR prohibition — Registry #2 caveat

amplify_threads.draft:
Yes — legal in most U.S. states, called credit card surcharging. Banned in MA, CT, and Puerto Rico. Card networks cap it at 3% and it only applies to credit, never debit.

GoDaddy Payments has a feature called Rate Saver that handles all state compliance automatically — opt in and your effective credit card processing rate drops to 0% [CLAIM: Registry #2]. Debit still processes at a reduced rate.

Available on paid plans only. Not for ecommerce (Online Store/WooCommerce).

Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments. Not legal advice — verify your state's rules.
amplify_threads.disclosure: Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments. Not legal advice.

amplify_threads.thread: How do I get out of my Clover contract early?
amplify_threads.draft:
Clover's 36-month lock-in through most resellers is one of the most common complaints here. Your options depend on how your contract was structured — check your Early Termination Fee and Term clauses in your original paperwork.

When you're ready to switch: GoDaddy Payments has no contract (month-to-month, cancel anytime [CLAIM: verify_needed]) and offers free migration support [CLAIM: verify_needed]. In-person rate 2.3% + $0 [CLAIM: Registry #1], no monthly software fee, 24/7 support on all plans.

godaddy.com/payments

Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments.
